vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure
physics_paths: Wing spar load path: wing_spar -> spar_socket -> fuselage_spine -> v_tail mount -> stabilizer
Avionics bay cooling: external air intake -> fuselage_spine vent -> avionics rack
Landing gear force: landing_gear_mount -> fuselage_spine -> wing spar socket
integration_opportunities: Potential for integrated wiring harness routing within fuselage spine
Opportunity to co-locate GPS and camera systems on the front boom

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces on spar socket and fuselage spine
manufacturing_strategy.support: Minimal support structures required due to box beam design
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Dimensional inspection using CMM, visual inspection for surface finish and cosmetic defects

verification_focus: Accessibility of internal components for maintenance

generated_parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

verify.value: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, and mating pads are visible and labeled.
verify.rule: good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.

verify.value: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
verify.rule: good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.

verify.value: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
verify.rule: good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

verify.value: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
verify.rule: good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

verify.value: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
verify.rule: good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.

verify.value: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible.
verify.rule: good: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible. | bad: Nacelle is a generic motor pod without tilt-axis evidence.

verify.value: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable.
verify.rule: good: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable. | bad: Wing/fuselage is a blended block with no interface features.

verify.value: Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details.
verify.rule: good: Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details. | bad: Design claims BVLOS capability, flight readiness, or actionable aircraft tuning.

verify.value: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers.
verify.rule: good: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers. | bad: Nacelle attaches by an undefined blend; motor and servo are entombed with no removal path or load fitting.

verify.value: cad_brief.mass_est_g = 350 g, target was <= 300 g.
verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Wing spar fatigue under repeated loading
risk.mit: Implement FEA analysis and stress testing.
Use locking nuts and torque to specification.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure
physics_paths: Lift: wing surface -> spar -> fuselage spine
Nacelle thrust: nacelle mount -> spar -> fuselage spine
Control: servo -> linkage -> control surface -> airflow
Thermal: leading edge -> internal channels -> trailing edge
integration_opportunities: Lightweighting: explore composite materials and topology optimization.
Sensor integration: consider mounting locations for pitot tubes, temperature sensors, etc.
verification_focus: Structural integrity: FEA analysis under load
Control surface movement: range of motion check
Mass budget: verify against target weight
